businessBackground: Criteo S.A. is a global commerce media technology company founded Nov 3, 2005 as a societe par actions simplifiee (S.A.S.) under French law, subsequently converted to a societe anonyme (S.A.). IPO October 2013. The company operates through two reportable segments: Performance Media (demand-side full-funnel commerce advertising, combining legacy Marketing Solutions and Iponweb SSP capabilities) and Retail Media (supply-side monetization platform for retailers).

V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07] **HELD at AVERAGE post-Q1.** Trajectory reset from "trough then H2 growth" to "transition year, Q4 growth at best." Q1 CeT $250M MET top of guide ✓ + EBITDA $65M ABOVE guide + margin guide held 32-34%, but FY26 CeT guide CUT to LSD decline (-300 bps midpoint), PM CeT -2% CC (78% of CeT segment), return-to-growth pushed Q4'26 (vs prior H2'26), NEW PM US-enterprise weakness (~150 bps; 8% LTM CeT bucket). Mgmt alignment two-step: (1) STRUCTURAL constraints favorable — $371M cash, $889M liquidity, no debt, $190M buyback authorization; French 10% buyback cap removes post Lux Q3'26. (2) ALIGNMENT signals mixed-positive — Chairman van der Kooi 3 open-market buys $423K (Nov'25-Mar'26 at $22.61/$21.52/$17.81), CFO RSU grant Mar'26 conditional on Lux redom; CEO Komasinski grant-sourced ownership only (no open-market purchases); CFO sold 61,347 sh @ $40.36 Nov'24. NOT WEAK because credible path to FY27 inflection still exists (RM lap Nov'26, OpenAI scaling, GO ramp at 2/3 of US small clients). NOT STRONG because PM (cash engine) just weakened. Council bear-case Model A 13.1% Compound-Failure Tail probability + epistemic-audit DRIFT detection (Q1 trough/H2 → Q4 recovery + 2027 reacceleration) keep score capped. (Sources: 8-K 2026-05-06; Q1'26 Prepared Remarks; OVERVIEW.md FY26 Outlook)

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07] **UPGRADE STRONG → EXTREME post-Q1.** At $15.64 (Source: prices.py 2026-05-07; -22% intraday post-Q1 reaction), EV $426M (mcap $797M − $371M net cash); **EV/TTM EBITDA 1.12× vs peer median 15× = 93% discount**. PW-FV $69.72 = **+346%**; bear FV $32.92 = +110% (no scenario-set downside); Owner Earnings cap @ 8% = $47.08 = +201%; Generalist FV (peer median × TTM, thesis-independent) $119.10 = **+662%**. Multi-method stress test: weakest reasonable method (4× bear) still +110% upside. Council market-inefficiency rated EXTREME ("market is pricing the thesis at negative value... mismatch robust across every reasonable parameterization"). Council fair-value PW-FV $68.8 (1.3% divergence vs Claude $69.72 = NOT material). Mismatch DEEPENED post-Q1: prior $18.50 anchor +173-305% → current $15.64 +335-602%.

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07; **REFRESHED 2026-05-14 Pattern-2-lens**] **DOWNGRADE 5 → 4 post-Q1 HELD; attribution refreshed.** V3 cap-below-EXTREME has TWO candidate drivers (not one, as of 2026-05-14): (a) original — PM US-enterprise client weakness TRANSIENT-UNPROVEN (~150 bps of FY26 guide cut, 8% LTM CeT bucket); (b) NEW — Pattern-2 ad-spend cannibalization (theme Risk #9): agent-routed demand reallocation could ACCELERATE PM-US-enterprise weakness if enterprise PM clients are reallocating to agent-routed channels OR could be INDEPENDENT if PM weakness is purely cyclical-macro. Q2'26 print resolves which attribution dominates: (a) PM weakness recurs without enterprise-clients citing agent-routed reallocation = cyclical-macro driver; (b) enterprise clients explicitly cite reallocation to ChatGPT / Adobe / Kargo ad-stacks = Pattern-2 mechanism active. Either way, V3 score holds 4/5 STRONG this session — the attribution narrative refines but numerical V-dim doesn't move. Market's bear case post-Q1 is articulable + partially valid: "structurally challenged ad-tech intermediary; FY25 was peak; FY26 guide cut signals further deterioration of PM (cash engine) via in-housing + take-rate compression + (NEW 2026-05-14) potential agent-routed cannibalization; -22% reaction is rational repricing." Drawdown Diagnosis (4 causes): B2/B1 RM scope reductions TRANSIENT with structural-watch trigger; **V1/D4 PM US-enterprise weakness TRANSIENT-UNPROVEN with Pattern-2 candidate driver** (resolution Q2-Q4'26; reclassify STRUCTURAL if PM declines >10% for 2Q or further guide cuts; Pattern-2 attribution test = enterprise clients explicitly cite agent-routed reallocation); A1/A2 domicile/index STRUCTURAL but addressed (Lux Q3'26 ✓, US Q1'27, Russell Jun'27); C1/B3 forced selling + category narrative TRANSIENT plus narrative. Mispricing mechanism still time-bounded but with one unproven element + one Pattern-2-candidate-driver element. (Sources: 8-K 2026-05-06; Q1'26 Prepared Remarks; Investor Presentation slide 23; CHANGELOG.md 2026-05-07; Theme `Themes/AI Disruption/THESIS.md` Risk #9 Pattern 2 2026-05-13; CRTO Pattern-2-lens revision CHANGELOG 2026-05-14)

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07] **HELD STRONG.** $371M cash + securities, $889M total liquidity, zero LT debt; at $15.64 mcap $797M, **net cash = 47% mcap (BETTER than prior 41%)**. $190M buyback authorization remaining; French 10% cap removes post Lux Q3'26. Lux dissenters' rights: EUR 17.94 floor / 10% cap / EUR 94.25M aggregate. Sub-archetype A "Balance Sheet as Weapon" applies; A6 leverage framework does not apply (net-cash). NOT EXTREME because: (a) cash floor $371M < mcap $797M (no hard-asset-floor exceeds price); (b) **LESSONS A21 applies** — council bear-case stress $10-18 (Model A 13.1%, range 10-18%) is materially wider than formal bear $32.92; asymmetry should NOT be treated as hard floor; (c) compound fragility risk (FP-7 FULL MATCH from bear-case agent) — correlated PM/RM/CapEx/take-rate erosion. 7-mode pre-mortem: PM structural decline (35-50% impairment); CapEx structural reset >$150M FY27 (15-30%); 3rd RM loss (20-30% + thesis reclassification); OpenAI commoditization (5-15% optionality erosion); buyback consumes cash into declining business (10-20%); litigation tail (2-10%); redom executes but fails to rerate (5-15%).

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07] **HELD AVERAGE.** 3 independent required conditions per council: (1) EBITDA/owner-earnings durability — **AT RISK** (Q1 print delivered, but FY26 EBITDA implied ~$382M vs FY25 $407M; PM weakness needs Q2-Q4 proof); (2) CapEx/FCF normalization — **UNCONFIRMED** (FY27 disclosure pending; <$120M = cyclical confirmation, >$150M = structural); (3) Rerating mechanism — **AT RISK** (Lux Q3'26 + buyback + Russell Jun'27 mechanical IF US Q1'27 hits Apr'27 rank day). NOT WEAK because base case doesn't require ALL upside paths to fire — any one of redom/buyback/M&A/visible recovery can drive modest multiple. NOT STRONG because Q1 guide cut demonstrated H2 return-to-growth slipped to Q4; correlated-cluster bear council framing applies.

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07; council rated 4/5 STRONG; Claude held 3/5 per strict rubric] **HELD AVERAGE.** RC 11/11; 19 transcripts + 9 filings + Q1 2026 8-K + Investor Presentation + factsheet + Investor FAQ + OpenAI press release processed. Q1 trough delivery RESOLVED near-term DATA GAP. BUT 6 critical forward-looking DATA GAPs persist + 2 NEW post-Q1: (1) PM US-enterprise client identity (NEW); (2) OpenAI exclusivity status (NEW — May press release silent); (3) FY27 CapEx normalization; (4) OpenAI revenue economics; (5) Shopper Graph data-source decomposition; (6) MGNI peer-multiple reconcile; (7) GO CeT contribution; (8) take-rate trajectory. **STRONG requires "No critical DATA GAPs"** per rubric — not satisfied. Per epistemic-audit, RC 11/11 partly reflects label management (HIGH_PRIORITY vs BLOCKING) — flagged CEREMONIAL. LESSONS A24: high research confidence ≠ asymmetric mispricing.

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.5, 2026-05-07] **HELD STRONG.** Q1'26 catalyst FIRED with mixed outcome (CeT $250M MET top of guide ✓ but FY26 guide cut). Replaced by dated chain: Q2 ~early Aug 2026 (CeT $260-264M guide); FY27 CapEx Q4'26-Q1'27 (resolves cyclical/structural debate); Q4'26 return-to-growth; Lux Q3'26 ✓ confirmed; US Q1'27 redom; Russell Jun'27 (mechanical IF US redom hits Apr'27 rank day). Self-correction tier: mostly EVIDENTIAL (preconditions in motion); Russell ARITHMETIC IF US redom eligible. NOT EXTREME because Q1 outcome mixed (cuts EXTREME's "ARITHMETIC self-correction" claim) + chain length ~14 months + return-to-growth pushed Q4'26. LESSONS A22 + A31 satisfied (timeline-matched, dated catalogue).

blindspots: **RM-PM Negative Flywheel** — If RM clients in-house, does Shopper Graph data flow continue under technology-only arrangements? If not, PM targeting quality degrades, creating correlated revenue decline across both segments. QUESTIONS.md has open item on Shopper Graph data source decomposition. Key question: what % of Shopper Graph comes from RM integrations vs publisher-side vs first-party? [COUNCIL: bear-case, o3, 2026-04-04]

**Publisher Fee-Share Retaliation** — As TAC/revenue falls (benefit to CRTO), publishers may demand higher floor pricing or switch supply paths, reversing margin gains. Second-order effect not tracked by any current indicator. Monitor: TAC ratio stabilization or reversal in FY2026 filings. [COUNCIL: bear-case, o3 updated-model, 2026-04-04]

**Litigation Tail Risk** — GoodRx settlement DENIED by court (Jan 2026); AlmondNet patent pending; CNIL EUR 40M appeal pending Conseil d'Etat (ruling expected 2026-27). Aggregate worst-case $15-20M (above $7M GAAP accrual). Precedent risk: if tracking pixel class actions succeed on merits, Criteo's data methodology may require restructuring. [QUESTIONS.md Filing Review; COUNCIL: bear-case, o3 v2]

**Ad Tech Base Rate Gap** — No sector-specific case study in vault. Historical base rate for profitable ad tech at sub-2x EV/EBITDA re-rating within 24 months is undefined. Council estimates range 15-30% depending on adjustment factors. DEBATES.md Vector 1 (Base Rate Attack) remains OPEN. [DEBATES.md 2026-03-30]

**SA360 Search Budget Diversion** — First SA360 campaign delivered 600% ROAS [FACT: Q4-25 transcript p.7]. If SA360 channels meaningful search budgets ($170-200B global market) into Retail Media, this is a material NEW growth vector not in thesis V6. No indicator currently tracks SA360-sourced CeT. Monitor Q1/Q2 2026 earnings for any disclosure. [COUNCIL: bull-optionality, o3 updated-model, 2026-04-04]

**DoorDash/Delivery Platform Exposure** — DoorDash acquired Symbiosys ($175M) for in-house ad-tech. Uber Eats US exited. What % of RM CeT comes from delivery/marketplace platforms vs traditional retail? Not disclosed. Delivery platforms have stronger in-housing incentives than traditional retailers. [DEBATES.md Vector 2]

noiseFilter: **Daily stock price / sector correlation moves** — CRTO is sub-$1B, thinly traded (4 analysts), moves with ad tech sentiment. Daily moves are noise. Only act on sustained (>10%) multi-week divergence from fundamentals.

**Analyst ratings without updated financial models** — Initiation or reiteration at current targets without revised EBITDA/CeT estimates adds no information. Only act on estimate revisions with explicit methodology change.

**OpenAI headline noise (TTD/Amazon partnership rumors, "ChatGPT ads" articles)** — Market conflates "OpenAI adds partners" with "Criteo loses." Thesis framing brief clarifies: Smartly = creative optimization (different function), NOT commerce demand. Criteo = sole commerce demand partner as of April 2026. Only act on CONFIRMED competing commerce DSP integration announcement with live ad inventory.

**Cookie deprecation narrative resurgence** — Google officially abandoned deprecation July 2024. Any revival is recycled bearish framing. Retargeting ~40% of PM but Criteo proactively diversified. Safari/Firefox already blocked — that's priced. Only act on ACTUAL new browser policy changes.

**Single-quarter CeT miss in Q1 2026** — Q1 is the GUIDED TROUGH ($245-250M). Missing by $5-10M is noise within the guided range. Only a miss below $230M with negative H2 commentary would be structural.
**SBC volatility quarter-to-quarter** — Iponweb LUS roll-off complete. Quarterly SBC fluctuates with grant timing and stock price. Only track annualized run-rate vs normalized expectation (~$55-65M).
